pdg_node.informal_desc: Converts time durations in milliseconds to the equivalent number of samples based on the sampling rate.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Calculates the effective amplitude range of the signal by creating a histogram and identifying the boundaries that exclude the top and bottom 1% of the signal distribution, effectively ignoring outliers.

declaration.name: estimate_signal_amplitude_range
declaration.docstring: Calculates the effective amplitude range of the signal by creating a histogram and identifying the boundaries that exclude the top and bottom 1% of the signal distribution, effectively ignoring outliers.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Normalizes the input signal to a [-1, 1] range based on the maximum absolute value of the estimated amplitude thresholds.

declaration.name: normalize_signal
declaration.docstring: Normalizes the input signal to a [-1, 1] range based on the maximum absolute value of the estimated amplitude thresholds.

declaration.name: normalize_signal
declaration.docstring: Normalizes the input signal to a [-1, 1] range based on the maximum absolute value of the estimated amplitude thresholds.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Identifies candidate R-peaks by finding points of high concavity in the normalized signal. This is achieved by locating the peaks of the inverted second derivative of the signal that exceed a given tolerance.

declaration.name: find_concave_candidate_peaks
declaration.docstring: Identifies candidate R-peaks by finding points of high concavity in the normalized signal. This is achieved by locating the peaks of the inverted second derivative of the signal that exceed a given tolerance.

declaration.name: find_concave_candidate_peaks
declaration.docstring: Identifies candidate R-peaks by finding points of high concavity in the normalized signal. This is achieved by locating the peaks of the inverted second derivative of the signal that exceed a given tolerance.

declaration.name: find_concave_candidate_peaks
declaration.docstring: Identifies candidate R-peaks by finding points of high concavity in the normalized signal. This is achieved by locating the peaks of the inverted second derivative of the signal that exceed a given tolerance.

pdg_node.statement: (candidate_peak_indices: np.ndarray, original_signal: np.ndarray, refractory_period_samples: int, search_window_samples: int) -> list[int]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Filters the candidate peaks by enforcing a minimum time separation between them (a refractory period of 300ms). For each valid candidate, it refines the peak location by finding the absolute maximum in the original signal within a 100ms window following the candidate index.

declaration.name: filter_and_refine_peaks
declaration.type_signature: (candidate_peak_indices: np.ndarray, original_signal: np.ndarray, refractory_period_samples: int, search_window_samples: int) -> list[int]
declaration.docstring: Filters the candidate peaks by enforcing a minimum time separation between them (a refractory period of 300ms). For each valid candidate, it refines the peak location by finding the absolute maximum in the original signal within a 100ms window following the candidate index.

declaration.name: filter_and_refine_peaks
declaration.type_signature: (candidate_peak_indices: np.ndarray, original_signal: np.ndarray, refractory_period_samples: int, search_window_samples: int) -> list[int]
declaration.docstring: Filters the candidate peaks by enforcing a minimum time separation between them (a refractory period of 300ms). For each valid candidate, it refines the peak location by finding the absolute maximum in the original signal within a 100ms window following the candidate index.

declaration.name: filter_and_refine_peaks

pdg_node.informal_desc: Ensures the final list of R-peak indices is unique and sorted in chronological order, then converts it to an integer numpy array.

declaration.name: finalize_rpeaks
